[?2004l]3008;start=d255d39c-4880-4b1a-8dc8-9e29fbe546b6;machineid=1da701bcb58248c7948b54ef4fc3afcf;user=ras;hostname=fedoralab;bootid=5ed2b3f1-e10e-4843-b8fa-3331fc75bd01;pid=00000000000000012912;type=command;cwd=/home/ras/linux/week10/wordpress/nginx\server {
	listen 80;
	server_name localhost;
	root /var/www/html;
	index index.php index.html;
	client_max_body_size 64M;
	location / { try_files $uri $uri/ /index.php?$args; }
	location ~ \.php$ {
		fastcgi_pass wordpress:9000;
		fastcgi_index index.php;
		include fastcgi_params;
		fastcgi_param SCRIPT_FILENAME $document_root$fastcgi_script_name;
		fastcgi_param PATH_INFO $fastcgi_path_info;
		fastcgi_read_timeout 300;
	}
	location ~* \.(js|css|png|jpg|jpeg|gif|ico|svg|woff|woff2)$ {
		expires 30d;
		add_header Cache-Control "public, no-transform";
		access_log off;
	}
	location ~ /\.(ht|git) { deny all; }
	# netdata
	location /nginx_status {
		stub_status on; allow 172.0.0.0/8; deny all;
	}
}

[?2004l]3008;start=33554896-1c1e-4455-b3fa-e91ef6f471e8;machineid=1da701bcb58248c7948b54ef4fc3afcf;user=ras;hostname=fedoralab;bootid=5ed2b3f1-e10e-4843-b8fa-3331fc75bd01;pid=00000000000000012912;type=command;cwd=/home/ras/linux/week10/wordpress\server {
	# override 127.0.0.1:80 -> 301 127.0.0.1:8443
	listen 80;
	server_name localhost;
	return 301 https://$host:8443$request_uri;
}

server {
	# enable SSL
	listen 443 ssl;
	server_name localhost;

	### SSL configuration block ###
	ssl_certificate /etc/nginx/certs/nginx-selfsigned.crt;
	ssl_certificate_key /etc/nginx/certs/nginx-selfsigned.key;
	ssl_protocols TLSv1.3;
	ssl_prefer_server_ciphers off;

	add_header X-Frame-Options "SAMEORIGIN";
	add_header X-Content-Type-Options "nosniff";
	add_header X-XSS-Protection "1; mode=block";
	add_header Strict-Transport-Security "max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains" always;
	add_header Referrer-Policy "strict-origin-when-cross-origin";
	### end SSL configuration block ###

	# Wordpress
	root /var/www/html;
	index index.php index.html;
	client_max_body_size 64M;
	location / { try_files $uri $uri/ /index.php?$args; }

	# Wordpress child
	location ~ \.php$ {
		fastcgi_pass wordpress:9000;
		fastcgi_index index.php;
		include fastcgi_params;
		fastcgi_param SCRIPT_FILENAME $document_root$fastcgi_script_name;
		fastcgi_param PATH_INFO $fastcgi_path_info;
		fastcgi_read_timeout 300;
	}

	# Wordpress Media
	location ~* \.(js|css|png|jpg|jpeg|gif|ico|svg|woff|woff2)$ {
		expires 30d;
		add_header Cache-Control "public, no-transform";
		access_log off;
	}

	# Force deny git
	location ~ /\.(ht|git) { deny all; }

	# netdata
	location /nginx_status {
		stub_status on; allow 172.0.0.0/8; deny all;
	}
}

# Please note that the parameters in this configuration file control the[m[2;73H[K[3;1H[34m# behavior of the tools from the shadow-utils component. None of these[m[3;71H[K[4;1H[34m# tools uses the PAM mechanism, and the utilities that use PAM (such as the

#
# Delay in seconds before being allowed another attempt after a login failure
# Note: When PAM is used, some modules may enforce a minimum delay (e.g.
#       pam_unix(8) enforces a 2s delay)

#FAIL_DELAY             3

# be updated.

#LASTLOG_UID_MAX

# Currently PORTTIME_CHECKS_ENAB is not supported

#SYSLOG_SG_ENAB         yes

# Currently CONSOLE is not supported[m[53;135H1,1[8C꼭대기[?25h[1;1H[?4m[?12$p[?25l[27m[23m[29m[m[H[2J[1;1H[96m#
# Please note that the parameters in this configuration file control the
# behavior of the tools from the shadow-utils component. None of these
# tools uses the PAM mechanism, and the utilities that use PAM (such as the
# passwd command) should therefore be configured elsewhere. Refer to

# Define the number of SHA rounds.[m[2;35H[K[3;1H[34m# With a lot of rounds, it is more difficult to brute-force the password.[m[3;74H[K[4;1H[34m# However, more CPU resources will be needed to authenticate users if
# this value is increased.
#
# If not specified, the libc will choose the default number of rounds (5000).
# The values must be within the 1000-999999999 range.
#
#SHA_CRYPT_MAX_ROUNDS 5000

# Currently SHA_CRYPT_MIN_ROUNDS is not supported

#
# Only works if ENCRYPT_METHOD is set to BCRYPT.
#
# Define the number of BCRYPT rounds.
# With a lot of rounds, it is more difficult to brute-force the password.
# However, more CPU resources will be needed to authenticate users if
# this value is increased.
#
# If not specified, 13 rounds will be attempted.
# If only one of the MIN or MAX values is set, then this value will be used.
# If MIN > MAX, the highest value will be used.
#
#BCRYPT_MIN_ROUNDS 13
#BCRYPT_MAX_ROUNDS 31

#
# Only works if ENCRYPT_METHOD is set to YESCRYPT.
#
# Define the YESCRYPT cost factor.
# With a higher cost factor, it is more difficult to brute-force the password.
# However, more CPU time and more memory will be needed to authenticate users
# if this value is increased.
#
# If not specified, a cost factor of 5 will be used.
# The value must be within the 1-11 range.
#
#YESCRYPT_COST_FACTOR 5

# Currently CONSOLE_GROUPS is not supported

#
# Should login be allowed if we can't cd to the home directory?
# Default is yes.
#
#DEFAULT_HOME   yes

# Currently ENVIRON_FILE is not supported
